143 - Why You Need to Qualify Referrals Before Sales Calls - YouTube.mp4
Now in this video, you're going to learn how to call referrals and how to have them instantly connect with you. I will tell you, referrals are a very powerful avenue of business for you if done properly. Remember, never assume that just because you get a referral that they're automatically going to be interested. Rule number one when calling referrals is to let of your attachment of making the sale and instead focus on whether there's a sale to be made in the first place remember the top 1% of all salespeople are the best at helping their potential customers find their problems and most importantly helping them solve their problems so find out if you can help them now the average salesperson assumes that just because this person's referral that somehow they're just automatically going to be interested in their solution here's what usually happens.
Let's take a look at the script here and let's just say, for example, you sell coaching services or maybe you even are a coach who does consulting for different companies or for different individual people. This would apply to you as well. So let's see what the average salesperson would say. Hi, is this Mary? Mary, this is... John Smith, with XYZ Coaching Company, Barry Goldwater asked me to call you as he said you'd be interested in my company's coaching services. As he's saying, you're wanting to take your consulting business to the next level, and you have three minutes right now where we can talk about how my company can you get your results and more profitable. Here we see the average salesman assuming that they're automatically interested just because their referral.
This will automatically put up sales resistance from your prospect and trigger sales pressure. The potential customer's defensive wall will come up soon with the traditional selling approach here. Now, let's say the prospect says something like this. Let's go back to the script. Yeah, I guess this is fine now. Average salesperson says, 'Okay, great.' I know you're going to be excited. About what I'm going to offer you today, you see here at XYZ Coaching Company, we've been in business for 10 years now. We've helped thousands of people build successful business businesses. And let me tell you a few things that we do to help people like you to get them where you're wanting to go. And then you can make an informed decision about working with us. Now I'm going to tell you.
When you try to use a closing technique and tell them that, after you do your pitch or talk about your features and benefits, and to them in a presentation, that after that they need to make an informed decision. Do you think your potential customers feel that you're trying to close them? What do you think they're going to be thinking most of the time in your presentation? Well, you're right. They'll only be thinking at the end of the presentation, this salesperson is going to try to close me on what they do. I wonder how much they're going to ask me to pay. What are some excuses I can say at the end to get out of this situation with the salesperson? Do you think they're going to be listening to your solution the whole time you're talking?
You see, you didn't even involve them in this process. You didn't even ask them any questions around what their problems are, if any, what's causing those problems. So under the surface... questions and how it's affecting them and their company, their family, their situation. Now let's take a look at what the seven-figure salesperson does. Now the seven-figure salesperson assumes nothing when he or she is about to call referral. Now to them, their first objective is to find out about the person and what problems they have, if any, and whether or not they can help them. Now let's look at a few ways to do this. The seven-figure salesperson will ask the person giving the referral this question. Let's look at the script.
Tell me, Barry, can you tell me more about this person and why you feel like I could help them? Very easy question. Doesn't it make sense to get as much background as you can about who you're about to call? It gives you a picture of the person and some reference points that you could possibly bring up when you call them. Here's an example. Let's go to the script. Hi Jane, this is Jeremy Miner, a mutual business associate of yours, Mr. Barry Goldwater, suggested that I give you a call. As I understand from him, you're possibly having a challenge. You would describe the challenge, and he thought that I might be able to help you. Is this an appropriate time to talk?
Now, most the time, the prospect is gonna say, 'Well, yeah, it's it's okay.' Why, because they don't feel any sales pressure from you. So they're more open from the very start the conversation. The seven-figure salesperson will say something like this. Okay, Jane, just so I don't go over things that Barry might have discussed with you, would you give me your thoughts on what you both already spoken about and what you'd like to cover so that we could focus on you and what you're possibly looking for? Now notice the softer language, the neutral stance. Notice the seven-figure salesperson uses the word 'possibly' a lot and 'maybe'. That is because when you're communicating with your potential customers, the seven-figure salesperson does not assume the sale. So when you stay neutral in the sales process, you...
when the trust of your prospect is, they don't feel sales pressure from you. You go from the dreaded salesperson in their eyes, the trusted authority or trusted advisor. They look at you as an advisor rather than just another salesperson trying to sell them something. You could also ask this question. Jane, it might be appropriate to both of us if I could understand a little bit more about you and what you're possibly looking for. Do you mind if I ask you a few questions? For example, what type of consulting business do you have now? And this is where you're going to start asking background questions, which I'm going to teach you in later videos. You are now in a two-way dialogue in discovering about their situation and if you can help them with what they do.
You are now well on your way to actually making the sale. That is what the seven-figure salesperson asked. Now, I want to thank you for watching this video about how to call referrals and how to have them instantly connect with you at the trust level. Now, in the next video, you're going to discover the shocking difference between traditional additional sales language, the language you might be currently using now, versus seven-figure sales language, the new model of selling for our era that we live in today.
